Yes, I believe Lone Wolf needs several changes.

First, the reset timer needs to be fixed and function correctly. Players need to know exactly when resets occur and be able to rely on the timer.

Second, the mode needs more variety. Instead of repeating the same questions and objectives over and over, create three unique sets of 20 levels and place them on a rotation. This would add diversity to the mode and help prevent it from becoming stale.

Titles should only change when the Lone Wolf mission set changes, not halfway through a rotation. Keeping title changes tied to season or mission rotations would make progression feel more consistent and understandable.

I would also like to see an additional competitive title added.

Pack Leader – Awarded to the player with the fastest Level 20 clear time.

Alpha Wolf (or another fitting title) – Awarded to the player with the fastest average completion time across all levels during a season.

I think Lone Wolf should operate on a 14-day season cycle. Every two weeks, the season would reset, rewards would become available again, leaderboards would refresh, and a new mission set could rotate in. This would give players a reason to keep returning while maintaining a healthy competitive environment.

Overall, Lone Wolf should focus on reliable resets, more mission variety, meaningful competition, and a consistent seasonal structure.
